Required Other language third-party pack: No additional third-party packages required beyond Laravel ecosystem
Logic Analysis: Creates user_feature_permission table with FK constraints to users, clients, features tables
Creates opt_pocket_expense_type table with seed data for expense types
Creates pocket_expense_source_client_config table with client_id FK and unique constraint
Creates pocket_expense table with FK to users, clients, opt_pocket_expense_type
Creates pocket_expense_metadata table with multiple FK constraints and unique constraint
Creates pocket_expense_file_uploads table for CSV upload tracking
Creates pocket_expense_uploads_data table for storing individual CSV row data
Factory for user_feature_permission table, depends on migrations
Factory for pocket_expense table with FK relationships
Eloquent model with relationships to User, Client, Feature models
Eloquent model for expense types lookup table
Eloquent model with client relationship and soft delete
Main expense model with multiple relationships and soft delete
Metadata model with multiple FK relationships
Authorization policy for user permission management, depends on models
Authorization policy for expense source management
Validation for granting user permissions, depends on policies
Validation for creating expenses with metadata
Business logic for RBAC operations, depends on models
Core expense CRUD operations with transaction handling
CSV validation logic with reference data preloading
FX conversion with wallet currency lookup and commission calculation
Background job for processing validated CSV data, depends on services
API response transformer for user permissions, depends on models
API response transformer for expenses with relationships
API response transformer for expense sources
API response transformer for upload tracking
RBAC API controller, depends on services + requests + resources
Main expense API controller with CRUD and approval operations
CSV upload API controller with validation and job dispatch
API route definitions with OAuth2 middleware groups, depends on controllers
Feature tests for RBAC functionality, depends on all application code + factories
Feature tests for expense CRUD operations
Feature tests for CSV upload and processing
Feature tests for expense source management
Full API spec:
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Volopa Out-of-Pocket Expenses API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: API for managing out-of-pocket expenses with RBAC, CSV uploads, and FX conversion
servers:
  - url: https://api.volopa.com/v1
    description: Production server
security:
  - OAuth2: []
components:
  securitySchemes:
    OAuth2:
      type: oauth2
      flows:
        clientCredentials:
          tokenUrl: /oauth/token
          scopes: {}
  schemas:
    UserFeaturePermission:
      type: object
      properties:
        id: {type: integer}
        user_id: {type: integer}
        client_id: {type: integer}
        feature_id: {type: integer}
        grantor_id: {type: integer}
        manager_user_id: {type: integer}
        is_enabled: {type: boolean}
    PocketExpense:
      type: object
      properties:
        id: {type: integer}
        uuid: {type: string}
        date: {type: string, format: date}
        merchant_name: {type: string}
        currency: {type: string}
        amount: {type: number}
        status: {type: string, enum: [draft, submitted, approved, rejected]}
    PocketExpenseSource:
      type: object
      properties:
        id: {type: integer}
        uuid: {type: string}
        name: {type: string}
        is_default: {type: boolean}
    UploadResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        success: {type: boolean}
        message: {type: string}
        upload_id: {type: integer}
        total_rows: {type: integer}
paths:
  /user-feature-permissions:
    get:
      summary: List user feature permissions
      parameters:
        - name: client_id
          in: query
          required: true
          schema: {type: integer}
      responses:
        200:
          description: Success
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/UserFeaturePermission'}
    post:
      summary: Grant user permission
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                user_id: {type: integer}
                client_id: {type: integer}
                feature_id: {type: integer}
                manager_user_id: {type: integer}
      responses:
        201:
          description: Permission granted
  /pocket-expenses:
    get:
      summary: List expenses
      parameters:
        - name: client_id
          in: query
          required: true
          schema: {type: integer}
      responses:
        200:
          description: Success
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/PocketExpense'}
    post:
      summary: Create expense
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                date: {type: string, format: date}
                merchant_name: {type: string}
                amount: {type: number}
                currency: {type: string}
                expense_type: {type: integer}
      responses:
        201:
          description: Expense created
  /pocket-expenses/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get expense
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: {type: integer}
      responses:
        200:
          description: Success
    put:
      summary: Update expense
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: {type: integer}
      responses:
        200:
          description: Updated
    delete:
      summary: Delete expense
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: {type: integer}
      responses:
        204:
          description: Deleted
  /pocket-expense-sources:
    get:
      summary: List expense sources
      parameters:
        - name: client_id
          in: query
          required: true
          schema: {type: integer}
      responses:
        200:
          description: Success
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/PocketExpenseSource'}
    post:
      summary: Create expense source
      responses:
        201:
          description: Source created
  /uploads/pocket-expense/csv:
    post:
      summary: Upload CSV file
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          multipart/form-data:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                file: {type: string, format: binary}
                user_id: {type: integer}
                expense_user_id: {type: integer}
                client_id: {type: integer}
      responses:
        200:
          description: Upload successful
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/UploadResponse'}
        422:
          description: Validation failed
Shared Knowledge: The system uses Laravel's built-in OAuth2 middleware (Oauth2UserClient) applied at route group level for authentication. All data operations are scoped by client_id for multi-tenancy. The system follows flag-based soft delete pattern with 'deleted' TINYINT(1) and 'delete_time' DATETIME columns. Timestamp columns use 'create_time' and 'update_time' instead of Laravel's default timestamps. Foreign key relationships are explicitly defined with constraints. The expense workflow follows draft->submitted->approved/rejected status flow. CSV uploads are processed asynchronously using Laravel queues with the ProcessExpenseUpload job. FX conversion integrates with existing platform /wallet-ccy-value endpoint. All API responses use API Resources for consistent formatting and field hiding.
Anything UNCLEAR: Several key implementation details require clarification: 1) The notification mechanism for batch upload completion (in-app, email, push, or combination) affects the ProcessExpenseUpload job implementation. 2) The ClientFeatures model structure and query pattern for checking if client has OOP Expense feature enabled (feature_id = 16) is needed for proper authorization. 3) The audit logging implementation for permission grants/revokes - whether to use existing platform audit infrastructure or create project-specific audit tables. 4) Consistency in soft delete convention - whether to use flag-based (deleted + delete_time) or Laravel SoftDeletes (deleted_at) pattern for new tables, as both appear in specifications.
